evidence: On January 2, 2025, we completed the acquisition of BrainBox AI Inc., a building management platform for HVAC optimization, using advanced AI technologies.
BrainBox AI Inc. is a building management platform for HVAC optimization, using advanced artificial intelligence technologies. The results of operations... are reported within the Americas segment.
The intangible assets associated with these acquisitions totaled $73.9 million and primarily relate to developed technology... combined total was $190.0 million.
Smart and AI-enabled services... Data center liquid cooling solutions... Data center HVAC systems listed as product offerings.
acquisition of BrainBox AI Inc., a building management platform for HVAC optimization, using advanced AI technologies
implementing emerging technological changes such as integrated AI solutions in our products and services

the rapid evolution and increased adoption of AI technologies may intensify our cybersecurity risks, including risks from malicious or misuse of AI to craft increasingly sophisticated cybersecurity attacks
AI also presents emerging issues and the pace presents uncertainties, and we may experience competitive harm, harm to our reputation, or legal liability.

limitations: The $73.9M / $190.0M figures cover multiple acquisitions (BrainBox AI plus European distributors), so the AI-specific portion is not separately quantified; investment intensity scored 3 rather than 4 due to this ambiguity.
No multi-year AI capex program or dedicated AI budget disclosed; score capped at 3.

Narrative concreteness scored 4 based on named deployed products (data center HVAC, AI-enabled services, BrainBox AI platform) but no quantified business results (e.g., revenue contribution, efficiency metrics) were disclosed to warrant a 5.

Risk section material is present in excerpts (labeled [business] risk-factor content and [risk_factors]); AI risks are discussed with company-specific mechanisms (cybersecurity attack vectors, competitive harm, product defects), supporting score 4, but no quantified risk exposure or named governance body was found to warrant a 5.
